title: Market Brief — Saturday, April 25, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes except H200. H100 availability sits at a severe 11% (109/339), B200 at 14% (192/449), while H200 is looser at 31% (75/336). The day's biggest move: H200 spot surged 22.5% to $3.48/hr, signaling a demand spike even in the most available tier. B200 utilization rocketed 36.4 percentage points in 30 days to 86.4%, confirming Blackwell absorption is real and accelerating.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes except H200. H100 availability sits at a severe 11% (109/339), B200 at 14% (192/449), while H200 is looser at 31% (75/336). The day's biggest move: H200 spot surged 22.5% to $3.48/hr, signaling a demand spike even in the most available tier. B200 utilization rocketed 36.4 percentage points in 30 days to 86.4%, confirming Blackwell absorption is real and accelerating.

Applying 1.25x TCO to raw GPU cost, Llama 70B inference on H100 runs $7.08/M and H200 runs $8.35/M. At medium-tier pricing ($3/M output), every provider routing through H100 or H200 is burning cash, losing roughly $4-5 per million tokens. B200 TCO at $3.65/M barely breaks even at medium tier. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a staggering loss against any Western GPU. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 compute is undervalued: 14% availability with the only positive inference margins across tiers, yet spot sits at just $4.00/hr versus $4.83 OCPI fair value. H200 is overvalued after today's 22.5% spike given 31% availability and collapsing utilization down 17.8pp in 30 days. Trade: go long B200 capacity, short H200 exposure.
bullets: 11% of H100 GPUs are available, making it the tightest compute market across all three classes.
192 of 449 B200 GPUs are free at 14% availability, a severe shortage despite being the newest chip.
22.5% is the single-day H200 spot price surge, the largest move in today's data.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 roughly 95% cheaper than GPT-4 era pricing, reflected in the LLMflation index hitting 21.41.
59.6% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable GPU asset to hold.
$975 per DDR5 32GB RDIMM module is up 4.84% week over week, signaling memory cost pressure on inference providers.
